between_subjects_variables.name: assigned_problem
between_subjects_variables.description: Birth sequence: Judge probability of birth order sequences. Program choice: Choose between programs based on student composition.

dependent_variables.name: representativeness_judgment
dependent_variables.measurement: Birth sequence: numerical estimate or comparative judgment. Program choice: categorical choice (A or B)

dependent_variables.name: proportion_showing_bias
dependent_variables.measurement: Proportion judging based on representativeness rather than statistical principles

participants.note: Total ~1500 across multiple studies; each participant answered ~24 questions from questionnaires

gender_distribution.note: Gender distribution not reported in original study

birth_sequence.description: Birth order sequence probability judgment task

program_choice.description: Student program selection based on class composition
program_choice.correct_answer: Program B (higher variance makes 55% more likely)

instructions.general: You will be presented with probability judgment problems. Please read each problem carefully and provide your best judgment.
instructions.task: For each problem, evaluate the probabilities or make choices based on the information provided.
instructions.response_format: Birth sequence: Provide numerical estimate or indicate comparative likelihood. Program choice: Select Program A or Program B.

procedure.description: Participants receive one problem from a questionnaire containing ~24 probability judgment questions. Each problem tests whether they judge based on representativeness (similarity to stereotype) rather than statistical principles. The birth sequence problem tests understanding of sample randomness. The program choice problem tests understanding of variance and regression to the mean.

steps.details: Participant reads the problem description with relevant statistical information
Birth sequence: Judge relative probabilities. Program choice: Select program A or B

manipulation_check.description: Verify participants understood the problem and probability concepts
questions: What were the two options presented?
Did you consider statistical principles in your answer?
expected_answers: Birth sequence: GBGBBG and BGBBBB. Program: Program A and Program B
